name: Sticky Appointment CTA
summary: A persistent appointment action that keeps the main conversion available after the user has enough trust and context.
useWhen: The page is long enough that the primary action can leave the viewport.
The user may be ready to act after reading proof, process, pricing, or FAQ content.
avoidWhen: The page already has competing primary actions.
The sticky action would obscure form fields, comparison data, or required legal content.

notes: Keep one primary appointment action and avoid duplicating every header link.
Document mobile placement and what content it must not cover.
Introduce after first proof or keep visually subordinate until the user has context.

findingLabels: Primary appointment action disappears after proof sections

confidence.reason: Reusable conversion support pattern; exact placement should be validated against content length and obstruction risk.
